faq.question: What time is it in Baghdad right now?
faq.answer: Baghdad operates on Arabia Standard Time (AST), UTC+3 year-round. The city maintains a consistent 3-hour offset ahead of Coordinated Universal Time throughout the year, with no daylight saving time adjustments since 2007.

faq.question: What time zone is Baghdad in?
faq.answer: Baghdad uses Arabia Standard Time (AST), UTC+3. Iraq permanently adopted this timezone in 2007 and does not observe daylight saving time, making Baghdad one of the few Middle Eastern capitals with year-round stable time that aligns with Saudi Arabia and Kuwait.

faq.question: Does Baghdad observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Iraq abolished daylight saving time in 2007 and has maintained Arabia Standard Time (UTC+3) continuously since then. This decision was made to reduce confusion and align with neighboring Gulf states.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Baghdad and New York?
faq.answer: Baghdad is 8 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (winter), and 7 hours ahead during Eastern Daylight Time (summer). When it's noon in New York, it's 8 PM in Baghdad during winter months.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Baghdad and London?
faq.answer: Baghdad is 3 hours ahead of London during Greenwich Mean Time (winter), and 2 hours ahead during British Summer Time (summer). This makes coordination with UK businesses relatively straightforward compared to transatlantic connections.

faq.question: What time does the stock exchange open in Baghdad?
faq.answer: The Iraq Stock Exchange (ISX) operates Sunday through Thursday from 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM AST (UTC+3). Trading sessions are shorter than Western exchanges and follow the regional workweek pattern of Sunday-Thursday rather than Monday-Friday.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Baghdad from the US?
faq.answer: From the US East Coast, call Baghdad between 6:00 AM and 10:00 AM EST to reach standard business hours (2:00 PM to 6:00 PM in Baghdad). From the West Coast, call between 3:00 AM and 7:00 AM PST for the same window.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Baghdad?
faq.answer: Government offices and businesses in Baghdad typically operate Sunday through Thursday from 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM AST, with many private companies working 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Friday and Saturday constitute the weekend in Iraq, following Islamic tradition.

seo_description: Current time in Baghdad, Iraq. Arabia Standard Time (AST) UTC+3 year-round. No daylight saving time. Get accurate timezone info and world clock conversions.

content_blocks.title: Understanding Baghdad's Arabia Standard Time Zone
content_blocks.content: Baghdad operates on Arabia Standard Time (AST) with a permanent UTC+3 offset throughout the entire year. Since Iraq abolished daylight saving time in 2007, the capital maintains consistent time alignment with Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Qatar. This stability simplifies international scheduling for businesses and travelers. The decision to abandon DST was driven by energy policy considerations and regional coordination needs. Baghdad's timezone places it exactly 3 hours ahead of London during winter (GMT) and 2 hours ahead during summer (BST), while maintaining an 8-hour lead over New York in winter and 7 hours in summer. For accurate time conversions between Baghdad and New York, use our specialized time difference with New York calculator. The consistent UTC+3 offset means sunrise and sunset times vary naturally with seasons without artificial clock adjustments, with sunrise typically occurring between 5:30 AM and 6:30 AM depending on the time of year.

content_blocks.title: Business Hours and International Collaboration from Baghdad
content_blocks.content: Baghdad follows a Sunday-through-Thursday workweek, reflecting Islamic business traditions across the Middle East. Government offices typically operate from 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM AST, while private sector companies often maintain 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM schedules. This creates a challenging but manageable overlap with European business hours, particularly London (9:00 AM Baghdad = 6:00 AM London in winter, 7:00 AM in summer). For US East Coast collaboration, there's a brief morning window when Baghdad afternoon (2:00 PM to 6:00 PM) overlaps with New York morning (6:00 AM to 10:00 AM EST). The Iraq Stock Exchange operates uniquely short sessions from 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM, Sunday through Thursday. International teams coordinating with Baghdad should account for Friday-Saturday weekends when scheduling conference calls. Our meeting planner tool helps identify optimal times across multiple timezones. Banking hours generally run 8:30 AM to 2:00 PM, with many services closing earlier on Thursdays. During Ramadan, business hours shift significantly earlier to accommodate fasting schedules.

content_blocks.title: Optimal Times for International Calls to Baghdad
content_blocks.content: Calling Baghdad from Western timezones requires strategic timing due to the significant offset. From New York (EST/EDT), the ideal window is 6:00 AM to 10:00 AM Eastern Time, reaching Baghdad during standard business hours of 2:00 PM to 6:00 PM. West Coast callers face a more challenging schedule, needing to dial between 3:00 AM and 7:00 AM PST/PDT. European callers have better alignment—London contacts can reach Baghdad professionals between 11:00 AM and 3:00 PM GMT/BST, hitting the 2:00 PM to 6:00 PM Baghdad window. For detailed scheduling between Baghdad and London, check our time difference with London page. When planning calls, remember Baghdad's weekend falls Friday-Saturday, not Saturday-Sunday. The lack of daylight saving time in Baghdad since 2007 means the time difference with DST-observing countries shifts twice yearly: Baghdad-New York gap changes from 8 hours (winter) to 7 hours (summer), while Baghdad-London varies between 3 hours (winter) and 2 hours (summer). Video conferences work best during the narrow overlap periods, typically late afternoon Baghdad time for transatlantic connections.
